title_en: Structure, Method, and Relationship to Earlier Works
content_en:
Usd al-Ghabah fi Ma'rifat as-Sahaba is organized alphabetically by first name, following the standard structure of Islamic biographical dictionaries. The work runs to five volumes in most modern editions and covers approximately 7,500 companion entries — significantly more than Ibn Abd al-Barr's Al-Isti'ab, reflecting both the supplementary scope of the work and Ibn al-Athir's access to additional sources that had become available in the two centuries between the two works.

Ibn al-Athir's stated method was to compile and synthesize five major earlier works on companion biography: Ibn Abd al-Barr's Al-Isti'ab, Ibn Mandah's Ma'rifat as-Sahaba, Abu Nu'aym al-Isfahani's Ma'rifat as-Sahaba, Ibn al-Basri's Al-Muhaddath, and Ibn Firil's Kitab as-Sahaba. By consulting and synthesizing these five sources — which had different strengths and different companion coverage — he could produce a more comprehensive reference than any single earlier work. He noted cases where the sources disagreed and identified companions not found in any of the five major sources.

The depth of treatment for individual companions varies significantly based on their historical importance and the available sources. Major companions — the first four caliphs, the ten promised paradise, the Prophet's closest family members and companions — receive extended entries incorporating all relevant information about their lives, Islamic service, hadith transmission, and death. Minor companions receive brief entries establishing their identity and status.

Ibn al-Athir's critical engagement with his sources is apparent throughout the work. He identifies companions who were incorrectly listed as such in earlier sources, notes mistakes in name spelling or lineage attribution, and points out cases where what appeared to be two different companions were in fact the same person under different names or spellings. This critical apparatus, while less rigorous by modern standards than we might wish, represents a genuine application of scholarly judgment to the traditional material. Usd al-Ghabah has remained the standard reference work for companion biography because it successfully balanced comprehensiveness with usability: comprehensive enough to cover the full known companion population, concise enough to remain manageable as a reference tool, and sufficiently rigorous in its source documentation to be trusted by subsequent scholars who built upon it. Al-Isabah fi Tamyiz as-Sahaba by Ibn Hajar, the definitive later work on companion biography, relied on Usd al-Ghabah extensively while refining and expanding its coverage — a relationship that confirms both the value and the limitations of Ibn al-Athir's contribution.
title_id: Struktur, Metode, dan Hubungannya dengan Karya-karya Sebelumnya
content_id:
Usd al-Ghabah fi Ma'rifat as-Shahabah disusun secara alfabetis berdasarkan nama pertama, mengikuti struktur standar kamus biografi Islam. Karya ini terdiri dari lima jilid dalam sebagian besar edisi modern, menampilkan sekitar 7.554 biografi Sahabat laki-laki dan perempuan.

Ibnu al-Atsir tidak menulis dari awal — beliau secara eksplisit menyebutkan bahwa karyanya merupakan sintesis dan perbaikan dari lima karya pendahulu utama: Kitab ash-Shahabah karya Ibnu Mandah, Al-Isti'ab fi Ma'rifat al-Ashab karya Ibnu Abd al-Barr, Kitab ash-Shahabah karya Abu Nu'aim al-Asfahani, Al-Mu'jam al-Kabir karya al-Thabarani, dan karya-karya lainnya. Kontribusi Ibnu al-Atsir adalah menggabungkan, mengoreksi, dan melengkapi sumber-sumber ini dengan informasi tambahan dari berbagai kitab hadis dan sejarah.
content_ar: Usd al-Ghabah fi Ma'rifat as-الصحابة is organized alphabetically by أولاً name, following the standard structure of Islamic biographical dictionaries. The work runs to five volumes in most modern editions and covers approximately 7,500 companion entries — significantly more than Ibn Abd al-Barr's Al-Isti'ab, reflecting both the supplementary scope of the work and Ibn al-Athir's access to additional sources that had become available in the two centuries between the two works. Ibn al-Athir's stated method was to compile and synthesize five major earlier works on companion biography: Ibn Abd al-Barr's Al-Isti'ab, Ibn Mandah's Ma'rifat as-الصحابة, Abu Nu'aym al-Isfahani's Ma'rifat as-الصحابة, Ibn al-Basri's Al-Muhaddath, and Ibn Firil's Kitab as-الصحابة. By consulting and synthesizing these five sources — which had different strengths and different companion coverage — he could produce a more comprehensive reference than any single earlier work. لاحظ cases where the sources disagreed and identified الصحابة not found in any of the five major sources. The depth of treatment for individual الصحابة varies significantly based on their historical importance and the available sources. Major الصحابة — the أولاً four caliphs, the ten promised الجنة, النبي ﷺ's closest الأسرة members and الصحابة — receive extended entries incorporating all relevant information about their lives, Islamic service, الحديث transmission, and الموت. Minor الصحابة receive brief entries establishing their identity and status. Ibn al-Athir's البالغ الأهمية engagement with his sources is apparent throughout the work. He identifies الصحابة who were incorrectly listed as such in earlier sources, notes mistakes in name spelling or lineage attribution, and points out cases where what appeared to be two different الصحابة were in fact the same person under different names or spellings. This البالغ الأهمية apparatus, بينما less rigorous by modern standards than we might wish, represents a genuine application of العلمي judgment to the التقليدي material. Usd al-Ghabah has remained the standard reference work for companion biography لأن it successfully balanced comprehensiveness with usability: comprehensive enough to cover the full known companion population, concise enough to remain manageable as a reference tool, and sufficiently rigorous in its source documentation to be trusted by subsequent العلماء who built upon it. Al-Isabah fi Tamyiz as-الصحابة by Ibn Hajar, the definitive later work on companion biography, relied on Usd al-Ghabah extensively بينما refining and expanding its coverage — a relationship that confirms both the value and the limitations of Ibn al-Athir's contribution.
